description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

v1.35.0

Myはんのう's top page has been reorganized into three sections: Daily / Weather / Services. Weather now has its own dedicated screen with more information, including graphs and moon phase data.

• Top split into 3 sections — Daily (garbage, events) / Weather / Services are now on separate screens so you can get straight to the information you need
• Always-visible header — The "Myはんのう" logo, date, weather icon (today's forecast), and service icons always appear at the top. Tap to scroll to each section. Tap "Myはんのう" to return to the top
• Low/high temperature below weather icon — See today's temperature at a glance
• Hourly temperature graph — A smooth 24-hour temperature curve overlaid on the heat index color band, with min/max values shown above their times
• Precipitation bar / heat index always shown — Even on rain-free or cool days, bars and labels are always visible so you know what you're looking at
• Moon phase track — At the bottom of the weather card, displays today's moon shape and name (new moon, crescent, first quarter, waning gibbous, full moon, waxing gibbous, last quarter). Horizontal axis shows the lunar cycle (left = new moon / center = full moon / right = next new moon)
• Moon details (tap to view) — Tap the moon icon (hover on PC) to see lunar age, moonrise/moonset, brightness, and distance from Earth. Supermoon days are highlighted with a badge
• Swipe left/right in weather section too — Use the same gesture as the daily section to compare past or upcoming weather
• Improved stability during horizontal swipe — The screen no longer jitters vertically while swiping horizontally
• PgUp / PgDn for section navigation — On PC keyboard, move between Daily / Weather / Services

---

v1.34.1

Minor schedule adjustments and update history page fixes.

• Stop time offsets within the same minute now 60-second divisions — When multiple stops fall in the same minute, spacing is 0/30 sec for 2 stops, 0/20/40 sec for 3, etc., always as whole seconds. All times are offset backward, so no "earlier than scheduled" notices appear
• Schedule times shown truncated to minute — Even if internal values include seconds, display always shows the scheduled minute
• Fixed update history page stuck at v1.23 — Corrected a parser bug that prevented /updates/ from showing the latest release
• Version number in header now links to history — Tap the version number at top left to jump to the update history page (/updates/)
• Footer version info simplified — Removed version numbers from page footers, keeping only build info (commit hash + build date)

---

v1.34.0

Bus features expanded.

• Bus stop map added — All 249 registered stops are displayed on a map. Marker size shows the number of routes per stop, making hub stations like Hanno Station (10 routes) and Higashi-Hanno Station (7 routes) instantly recognizable. Access from the bus screen via "🗺️ Find on map"
• "Find on map" everywhere — Jump to the map from the boarding/destination stop selection screen, the "Change" button, or the main menu to select stops
• Directions and routes in geographic order — Bus directions and route lists now sort from north to south and west to east (previously by frequency)
• Nagiyanagi Loop displays full loop — Selecting "Get on here" → "Nagiyanagi direction" shows the full circuit: leave Hanno Station, loop through Nagiyanagi and Kojo-mae, return to Hanno Station at stop 22
• Fixed stop order for northbound (Hanno-bound) services — Stops recorded at the same minute (e.g., Naguri Depot and Tsukue) now appear in correct order
• First departure time shows as scheduled — Changed same-minute adjustments to backward-only, eliminating "earlier than scheduled" displays
• Hanno City Hall destination now finds Nagiyanagi Loop — Setting the destination to Hanno City Hall in "Go here" now shows the Nagiyanagi Loop as a candidate

⚠️ Bus URLs have changed. Bookmarks to /@hanno/bus/out/ and /@hanno/bus/in/ no longer work. Use /@hanno/bus/from/ and /@hanno/bus/to/ instead.

---

v1.33.0

Bus times and stops made more accurate.

• Bus arrival time at final destination now shown — The arrival time at your destination (final stop) is now displayed. For stops not in the schedule, arrival time is estimated from the actual travel time of services covering the same section
• Fixed stop order for northbound (Hanno-bound) services — Stops recorded at the same minute (e.g., Naguri Depot and Tsukue) now appear in the correct sequence
• Nagiyanagi Loop routes now distinguished — Shown as "Nagiyanagi Loop (via Kojo-mae first)" and "(via Hanno-Shouwa first)" so you know which direction the bus goes
